key_insight: Sintering route strongly controls bulk TiO2 permittivity and loss, and SPS plus post-annealing can produce THz-suitable ceramics with ε≈103 and very low loss.
engineering_relevance: These TiO2 ceramics meet the high-permittivity, low-loss requirements needed for terahertz all-dielectric metamaterials with negative or near-zero effective index.
method: Compared conventional sintering (CS) against spark plasma sintering (SPS).
Applied post-sintering annealing to reduce dielectric loss.
Characterized bulk pellets with THz time-domain spectroscopy (THz-TDS) and used the measured properties for metamaterial design.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports one sample with permittivity ε = 103 and loss tangent as low as 0.006, which satisfies the stated target criteria.
watch_item: Dielectric performance is highly process-dependent, so fabrication consistency and scaling remain critical before device integration.
paper_title: Impact of sintering conditions on the dielectric properties of TiO2 ceramics for metamaterialsapplications at terahertz frequencies
